name: Ohio SNAP / Food Assistance (Direction Card)
description: Provides monthly food benefits via EBT card (Ohio Direction Card) to eligible low-income individuals and families for purchasing groceries. Administered by ODJFS through county offices.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents with gross income at or below 200% of Federal Poverty Guidelines. Must be U.S. citizens or qualified non-citizens.
incomeLimitNotes: 200% FPG: 1 person $2,514/mo; 2 persons $3,408/mo; 3 persons $4,302/mo; 4 persons $5,196/mo. Able-bodied adults may have work requirements of 20 hrs/week.
hours: Online applications accepted 24/7. County JFS office hours vary.

name: Ohio Medicaid
description: Provides free or low-cost health coverage including doctor visits, hospital stays, prescriptions, mental health services, and long-term care for eligible low-income Ohioans.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents who are U.S. citizens or qualified non-citizens. Covers adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities.
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 206% FPL; pregnant women up to 200% FPL. Long-term care income limit $2,982/month (2026).
hours: Online applications 24/7. Medicaid Consumer Hotline hours vary.

name: Ohio Medicaid Consumer Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing information about Ohio Medicaid managed care plans, enrollment, plan selection, and connecting members with their health plan (CareSource, Molina, Buckeye, etc.).
eligibilitySummary: Any Ohio Medicaid member or applicant seeking information about managed care plans.
hours: Monday-Friday, business hours. TTY: 711

name: Ohio Unemployment Insurance (ODJFS)
description: Provides temporary income replacement benefits to eligible workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio workers who lost employment through no fault of their own, earned sufficient wages during a base period, and are able and available to work.
incomeLimitNotes: Benefits based on prior earnings. Must have earned minimum qualifying wages. Weekly certifications required.
hours: Online filing available 24/7. Phone: TTY 1-888-642-8203

name: OhioMeansJobs
description: Ohio's premier online career and employment center offering job search, resume building, career assessments, training opportunities, GED practice tests, and connections to local workforce development centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Ohio job seekers, employers, students, veterans, and workers with disabilities. Free to use.
hours: Online resources available 24/7. Local OhioMeansJobs center hours vary by county.

name: Opportunities for Ohioans with Disabilities (OOD)
description: State agency providing vocational rehabilitation services to help individuals with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, job training, placement assistance, and assistive technology.
address: 150 E. Campus View Blvd., Columbus, OH 43235
eligibilitySummary: Ohioans with physical, intellectual, mental health, or sensory disabilities who need help finding or keeping employment.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Local offices throughout Ohio.

name: Ohio Housing Finance Agency (OHFA)
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable housing programs including rental assistance, down payment assistance, mortgage tax credits, and housing development financing for low- and moderate-income Ohioans.
eligibilitySummary: Low- to moderate-income Ohio residents. Specific eligibility varies by program (homebuyer, renter, developer).
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program. First-time homebuyer programs have county-specific income and purchase price limits. 811 PRA for extremely low-income disabled renters.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: OHFA Down Payment Assistance (Your Choice!)
description: Provides 2.5% or 5% of the home purchase price as down payment and closing cost assistance through forgivable or repayable second mortgage options for eligible first-time homebuyers in Ohio.
eligibilitySummary: First-time homebuyers or buyers in targeted areas. Must meet income and purchase price limits, complete homebuyer education, and use an OHFA-approved lender.
incomeLimitNotes: Income and purchase price limits vary by county. Check myohiohome.org for current limits.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Ohio Legal Help
description: Free statewide website providing plain-language legal information, self-help tools, court forms, and connections to local legal aid organizations for Ohioans facing civil legal problems.
eligibilitySummary: Free for all Ohio residents. Covers family law, housing, consumer/debt, health benefits, employment, and seniors' legal issues.
hours: Website available 24/7. Legal Aid referral line hours vary.

name: Legal Aid Society of Cleveland
description: Provides free civil legal representation to low-income individuals in consumer rights, domestic violence, education, employment, family law, health, housing, immigration, public benefits, utilities, and tax law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Ashtabula, Cuyahoga, Geauga, Lake, and Lorain Counties. Income eligibility guidelines apply.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% of Federal Poverty Guidelines or below.
hours: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Legal Aid of Southeast and Central Ohio (LASCO)
description: Provides free noncriminal legal help for low-income individuals, veterans, and seniors in 36 Ohio counties covering southeast and central Ohio regions.
address: 1108 City Park Avenue, Suite 200, Columbus, OH 43206
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents, veterans, and seniors in 36 counties across southeast and central Ohio.
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% of Federal Poverty Guidelines or below.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Disability Rights Ohio
description: Ohio's Protection and Advocacy system providing free legal advocacy for people with disabilities on issues including abuse, neglect, discrimination, education, housing, employment, and community integration.
address: 200 Civic Center Drive, Suite 300, Columbus, OH 43215
eligibilitySummary: Ohioans with disabilities facing legal issues related to their disability including abuse, neglect, discrimination, and access to services.
hours: Intake hours: Monday-Friday, 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM. Local: 614-466-7264

name: Ohio Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP)
description: Federally funded program providing a one-time benefit to help eligible low-income Ohioans pay home heating bills. Benefit is applied directly to utility or bulk fuel account.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents with gross household income at or below 175% of Federal Poverty Guidelines. Both homeowners and renters eligible.
incomeLimitNotes: 175% FPG. For households of 9+ members, income must be at or below 60% State Median Income.
hours: Applications accepted July through May 31. Hearing impaired: dial 711.

name: Ohio Winter Crisis Program (Emergency HEAP)
description: Emergency heating assistance for income-eligible Ohioans who have been disconnected, received a shut-off notice, or have less than 25% supply of bulk fuel. Can also assist with fuel tank and heating system repair.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents at or below 175% FPG who have been disconnected, received a disconnection notice, or have less than 25% bulk fuel supply.
incomeLimitNotes: 175% of Federal Poverty Guidelines. One-time assistance per heating season.
hours: Available November 1 through March 31 annually.

name: PIPP Plus (Percentage of Income Payment Plan)
description: Year-round payment plan allowing eligible customers to pay electric and/or natural gas bills based on a percentage of household income (6-10%), with debt forgiveness after 24 on-time payments.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio customers of regulated electric or natural gas utilities with household income at or below 175% of Federal Poverty Guidelines.
incomeLimitNotes: 175% FPG. Gas-heated homes: 6% of income for gas, 6% for electric. Electric-heated homes: 10% of income. Minimum payment $10/month.
hours: Applications accepted year-round at energyhelp.ohio.gov

name: United Way 2-1-1 Ohio
description: Free, confidential information and referral service connecting callers to local social services including food, housing, utilities, healthcare, employment, childcare, crisis counseling, and disaster assistance 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all Ohio residents seeking help finding social services and community resources.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year

name: Greater Cleveland Food Bank
description: Northeast Ohio's largest hunger-relief organization distributing food to 1,000+ food pantries, meal programs, shelters, and nonprofits. Serves over 404,000 people across six counties.
eligibilitySummary: Open to individuals and families in need of food assistance in Cuyahoga, Ashtabula, Geauga, Lake, Ashland, and Richland counties.
hours: Help Center: 216-738-2067. Office hours vary by location.

name: Mid-Ohio Food Collective
description: Ohio's largest food bank working with 600+ local charities and programs to provide fresh, healthy food across 20 central Ohio counties. Also connects neighbors with job training, housing, and childcare resources.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in 20 central Ohio counties.
hours: Varies by program and distribution site.

name: Freestore Foodbank (Cincinnati)
description: Greater Cincinnati's largest emergency food and services provider, distributing 47.2 million meals annually across 20 counties in Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana. Operates Liberty Street Market choice pantry.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in Greater Cincinnati tri-state area (20 counties in OH, KY, IN).
hours: Customer Connection Center: 112 E. Liberty Street, Cincinnati, OH 45202. Hours vary.

name: Akron-Canton Regional Foodbank
description: Feeding America member food bank distributing food to 600 hunger-relief programs including food pantries, meal sites, shelters, and children/senior programs across eight Northeast Ohio counties.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need in Carroll, Holmes, Medina, Portage, Stark, Summit, Tuscarawas, and Wayne counties.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Toledo Northwestern Ohio Food Bank
description: Feeding America member food bank serving approximately 250 nonprofit agencies across eight northwestern Ohio counties. Mission to feed, educate, and empower communities to end hunger.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in Defiance, Fulton, Henry, Lucas, Ottawa, Sandusky, Williams, and Wood counties.
hours: Monday-Friday, business hours. Distribution schedules vary by agency.

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of the Mahoning Valley
description: Feeding America member food bank serving hunger-relief organizations across three counties in the Mahoning Valley area. Programs include mobile pantry, backpack programs, and nutrition education.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in Columbiana, Mahoning, and Trumbull counties.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Ohio Association of Foodbanks
description: Statewide network representing Ohio's 12 Feeding America foodbanks and 3,600 hunger-relief agencies. Coordinates SNAP outreach, agricultural clearance programs, and healthcare enrollment assistance.
address: 101 E. Town Street, Suite 540, Columbus, OH 43215
eligibilitySummary: Serves all Ohioans in need through its network of 12 regional food banks and 3,600 local agencies.
hours: Monday-Friday, business hours

name: Ohio Works First (OWF) / TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary cash assistance program for families with minor children. Provides monthly cash benefits while adults participate in work activities, job training, or education for up to 36 months.
eligibilitySummary: Families with minor children who meet income and resource limits. Adults must participate in work activities 20-35 hours per week.
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below program limits. Benefits limited to 36 months unless hardship extension is approved. Benefits via EPPIcard or direct deposit.
hours: Online applications 24/7. County JFS offices: Monday-Friday, business hours.

name: Ohio Prevention, Retention & Contingency (PRC) Program
description: County-administered emergency assistance for families with minor children facing unexpected crises. Provides one-time, short-term help with rent, utilities, car repair, work-related expenses, and other barriers to self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with minor children facing a crisis or barrier to employment. Each county has its own PRC plan with specific eligibility rules.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county. Generally serves families below 200% FPL. Benefits are non-recurrent and short-term.
hours: County JFS offices: Monday-Friday, business hours.

name: Ohio Disability Financial Assistance (DFA)
description: Monthly cash benefit for low-income persons with disabilities who do not qualify for SSI or other federal/state programs. Maximum benefit of $115/month per individual.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents determined disabled by ODJFS who do not qualify for SSI or Ohio Works First.
incomeLimitNotes: Family resources cannot exceed $1,000. Maximum monthly benefit $115 for one person, increases for additional disabled household members.
hours: Online applications 24/7. County JFS offices: Monday-Friday, business hours.

name: Ohio WIC Program (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Provides supplemental nutrition assistance, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for eligible pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children under 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers (up to 1 year postpartum), postpartum women (up to 6 months), infants, and children under 5 with nutritional risk.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of Federal Poverty Level. Automatically eligible if receiving Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF.
hours: WIC clinics located in all 88 Ohio counties. Hours vary by clinic.

name: Ohio Publicly Funded Child Care (PFCC)
description: Subsidizes child care costs for low-income working parents or parents enrolled in approved education/training programs. Administered through county JFS offices.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children who are working, in school, or in approved training. Must be Ohio residents.
incomeLimitNotes: PFCC: up to 145% FPL. Child Care Choice Program: 146-200% FPL. Copayments may apply based on income.
hours: County JFS offices: Monday-Friday, business hours.

name: Ohio Department of Veterans Services (ODVS)
description: State agency connecting veterans and families with benefits, employment, housing, and healthcare services. Oversees Ohio Veterans Homes and provides training to 88 County Veterans Service Offices.
address: 77 S. High Street, 7th Floor, Columbus, OH 43215
eligibilitySummary: Ohio veterans and their families. Specific eligibility varies by program (Veterans Homes, Veterans Bonus, education programs).
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM

name: COTA Income Assistance Program (Columbus)
description: Provides 50% discounted bus fares for low-income riders enrolled in public assistance programs (Medicaid, TANF, WIC, SNAP). Part of Central Ohio Transit Authority's fare equity initiative.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals receiving public income assistance such as Medicaid, TANF, WIC, or SNAP/EBT in the COTA service area.
incomeLimitNotes: Must show proof of enrollment in qualifying assistance program. Maximum daily charge $2.25; monthly cap $31.
hours: Customer Experience Center hours vary. Transit service operates daily.

name: Greater Cleveland RTA Discount Fare Programs
description: Reduced transit fares for seniors (65+), persons with disabilities, students, and ADA paratransit riders on all RTA bus and rail services throughout Greater Cleveland.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 65+, Medicare cardholders, persons with verified disabilities, students K-12 and college (U-Pass), and children under 6 (free). ADA paratransit riders ride fixed routes free.
hours: Transit service operates daily. Customer service hours vary.

name: Franklin County Department of Job and Family Services
description: Provides workforce development, SNAP, Medicaid, cash assistance, child care assistance, PRC emergency aid, and other family support programs for Franklin County (Columbus) residents.
eligibilitySummary: Franklin County residents applying for public assistance, employment services, or emergency aid programs.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program (SNAP, Medicaid, OWF, PRC each have different income thresholds).
hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 AM - 5:30 PM

name: Franklin County PRC Emergency Assistance
description: Emergency one-time assistance for Franklin County families with minor children. Helps with rent, utilities, car repair, employment-related costs, and other barriers to self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Franklin County families with minor children facing a crisis or barrier to employment/self-sufficiency.
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits per Franklin County PRC plan. Short-term, non-recurring assistance only.
hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 AM - 5:30 PM

name: Columbus WIC Program
description: Columbus Public Health-operated WIC program providing nutrition assistance, education, and referrals for eligible pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children under 5 in Franklin County.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant women, postpartum/breastfeeding women, infants, and children under 5 in Franklin County with nutritional risk.
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% FPL. Auto-eligible if on Medicaid, SNAP, or TANF.
hours: Monday-Friday. Call for appointment.

name: Ohio Community Action Agencies (OACAA Network)
description: Network of 48 Community Action Agencies serving all 88 Ohio counties with programs addressing poverty including emergency services, job training, financial literacy, housing, energy assistance, and family development.
address: 175 S. Third Street, Suite 350, Columbus, OH 43215
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals, families, children, and seniors. Eligibility varies by program; generally serves those at or below 200% FPL.
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program. Most programs target households below 125-200% of Federal Poverty Guidelines.
hours: Varies by local agency. Contact your county's Community Action Agency.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (Ohio)
description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support for anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or substance use crisis. Ohio has 19 call centers answering locally with 99% of contacts handled by Ohio operators.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Ohio experiencing a suicidal, mental health, or substance use crisis. Free and confidential.
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Call, text, or chat.

name: Ohio Dental OPTIONS Program
description: Connects eligible low-income, uninsured adults with volunteer dentists providing donated or discounted dental care. Administered by the Ohio Department of Health with Dental Lifeline Network.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income, uninsured Ohio adults who do not qualify for Medicaid and have minimal financial means or severe dental needs. Also serves elderly and people with disabilities.
incomeLimitNotes: Income eligibility determined through pre-screening by Ohio Department of Health.
hours: Call for pre-screening and dentist matching.

name: Ohio Access to Justice Foundation
description: Funds Ohio's legal aid organizations and access-to-justice initiatives including Ohio Legal Help, the Ohio Justice Bus, and local legal aid societies to expand civil legal services for low-income Ohioans.
address: 100 E. Broad Street, Suite 500, Columbus, OH 43215
eligibilitySummary: Funds legal aid programs statewide. Individual clients access services through local legal aid organizations.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Cuyahoga County Veterans Service Commission
description: Provides emergency financial assistance, transportation to VA appointments, claims assistance, and referral services for veterans and their families in Cuyahoga County (Cleveland area).
eligibilitySummary: Veterans and dependents residing in Cuyahoga County who meet program-specific eligibility requirements.
incomeLimitNotes: Financial assistance has income/asset guidelines. VA claims assistance available regardless of income.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM

name: Step Forward (Cuyahoga County Community Action Agency)
description: Cuyahoga County's Community Action Agency providing HEAP/PIPP energy assistance, weatherization, workforce development, financial coaching, and housing programs for low-income residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of Cuyahoga County. Eligibility varies by program.
incomeLimitNotes: HEAP/PIPP: 175% FPL. Other programs vary. Contact for specific eligibility.
hours: Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM

name: Franklin County Child Care Assistance
description: Subsidized child care for eligible working families and families in education/training programs in Franklin County. Administered through Franklin County JFS with multiple provider options.
eligibilitySummary: Franklin County families with children who are working or in approved training/education. Must meet income requirements.
incomeLimitNotes: PFCC: up to 145% FPL. Child Care Choice: 146-200% FPL. Copayments based on income and family size.
hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 AM - 5:30 PM

name: Ohio SNAP and Food Assistance Legal Help
description: Free legal information and assistance for Ohioans with SNAP/food assistance issues including denied applications, benefit reductions, overpayment claims, and administrative hearings.
eligibilitySummary: Ohio residents experiencing legal issues with SNAP or food assistance programs.
hours: Website available 24/7. Legal Aid phone referral hours vary.
